DISCOVERY, DEPT. 525, AS TO THE DEC-05-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS GRUBMARKET, INC., AND FENGLIN YU'S MOTION BY OF MOTION BY DEFENDANTS FOR LEAVE TO RE-OPEN DISCOVERY TO ALLOW FOR DEPOSITION OF NOEMI VELARDE, WIFE OF PLAINTIFF ALFREDO VELARDE; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION IS DENIED. JUDGE FENG'S SEPTEMBER 20, 2024 ORDER PRECLUDES FURTHER DISCOVERY AS TO NON-EXPERT WITNESSES SUCH AS MS. VELARDE. COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, AND EMAIL IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM TOM COHEN AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM . JUDGE PRO TEM: TOM COHEN; CLERK: V. DA FONSECA; NOT REPORTED. =(525/JPT)

DISCOVERY, DEPT. 525, AS TO THE DEC-05-2024 HEARING RE: DEFENDANTS GRUBMARKET, INC., AND FENGLIN YU'S MOTION FOR LEAVE TO RE-OPEN DISCOVERY TO ALLOW FOR INTERROGATORIES TO IDENTIFY HEALTH CARE PROVIDERS NOT PREVIOUSLY IDENTIFIED; THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING. THE MOTION TO REOPEN DISCOVERY IS DENIED. HOWEVER, PLAINTIFF'S FAILURE TO ACCURATELY SUPPLEMENT HIS INTERROGATORY RESPONSES MAY BE USED AS IMPEACHMENT. CCP 2030.310(A); OR DEFENDANT MAY, AFTER MEETING AND CONFERRING, BRING A MOTION TO HOLD THE ORIGINAL ANSWER BINDING. CCP 2030.310(B). COUNSEL FOR THE PREVAILING PARTY IS TO PREPARE A PROPOSED ORDER WHICH REPEATS VERBATIM THE SUBSTANTIVE PORTION OF THE COURT'S RULING, AND EMAIL IT TO JUDGE PRO TEM TOM COHEN AT TOM@THOMASCOHEN.COM . Amended Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Re-Open Discovery To Allow The Deposition Of Plaintiffs Employer
Off Calendar Dec-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS GRUBMARKET, INC., AND FENGLIN YU's Motion For Leave To Re-Open Discovery To Allow The Deposition Of Plaintiffs Employer.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

This motion appears to be identical to the one seeking discovery as to Mr. Malfatti. Plaintiff's previous employer. That motion was decided on December 14, 2024.

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. =(525/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Of Motion By Defendants For Leave To Re-Open Discovery To Allow For Deposition Of Noemi Velarde, Wife Of Plaintiff Alfredo Velarde
Off Calendar Dec-05-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS GRUBMARKET, INC., AND FENGLIN YU's Motion By Of Motion By Defendants For Leave To Re-Open Discovery To Allow For Deposition Of Noemi Velarde, Wife Of Plaintiff Alfredo Velarde.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The Motion is denied. Judge Feng's September 20, 2024 Order precludes further discovery as to non-expert witnesses such as Ms. Velarde.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, and email it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com .

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. =(525/JPT)

Notice Of Motion And Motion By Defendants For Leave To Re-Open Discovery To Allow For Mental Examination And Neuropsychological Testing And To Compel Attendance At Neuropsychological Examinaton
Off Calendar Dec-05-2024 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS GRUBMARKET, INC., AND FENGLIN YU's Motion By Defendants For Leave To Re-Open Discovery To Allow For Mental Examination And Neuropsychological Testing And To Compel Attendance At Neuropsychological Examination.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion is granted. Good cause based on newly revealed evidence warrants reopening discovery to allow for Mental Examination and Neuropsychological Testing. The parties shall cooperate to find a mutually agreeable time and place for such examination.

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, and email it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com .

Judge Pro Tem: Tom Cohen; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. =(525/JPT)

MOTION FOR LEAVE TO To Re-Open Discovery To Allow For Interrogatories To Indentify Health Care Providers Not Previously Identified
Off Calendar Dec-05-2024 Denied
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANTS GRUBMARKET, INC., AND FENGLIN YU's MOTION FOR LEAVE To Re-Open Discovery To Allow For Interrogatories To Identify Health Care Providers Not Previously Identified.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

The motion to reopen discovery is denied. However, Plaintiff's failure to accurately supplement his interrogatory responses may be used as impeachment. CCP 2030.310(a); or Defendant may, after meeting and conferring, bring a motion to hold the original answer binding. CCP 2030.310(b).

Counsel for the prevailing party is to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the Court's ruling, and email it to Judge Pro Tem Tom Cohen at tom@thomascohen.com .
